patch.value:
## Context

The runtime field system is fully implemented:
- schema/fields.json is loaded and merged by loadRuntimeSchemaFromOptionalFiles (src/core/schema/runtime-schema.ts:514-602)
- RuntimeFieldDefinition is validated and resolved by normalizeRuntimeFieldDefinition (runtime-schema.ts:238-278)
- Runtime field flags are dynamically registered on create/update/list/search/context commands (src/cli/main.ts:785-808)
- Fields can specify type (string/number/boolean/string_array), commands, required_on_create, allow_unset, cli_flag, cli_aliases

But:
- pm schema list (SchemaListResult) does NOT include a 'fields' section (src/cli/commands/schema.ts:163-187)
- SCHEMA_SUBCOMMANDS (schema.ts:42-51) = ['add-type','remove-type','add-status','remove-status','list','show','show-status'] — no field subcommands
- No field-specific lock like SCHEMA_TYPES_LOCK_ID/SCHEMA_STATUSES_LOCK_ID exists for fields
- CONFIGURATION.md has zero mention of schema/fields.json or RuntimeFieldDefinition

## Acceptance criteria

- pm schema list includes a 'fields' section showing registered custom fields (key, type, commands, cli_flag)
- pm schema show-field <key> shows full field definition
- pm schema add-field <key> --type <type> --commands create,update --description <text> [--required] [--required-on-create] [--allow-unset] [--cli-flag <flag>] [--alias <alias>] manages schema/fields.json
- pm schema remove-field <key> removes entry from schema/fields.json with advisory usage count
- pm schema add-field is idempotent (upsert); warns if key shadows a builtin field name
- CONFIGURATION.md documents schema/fields.json format and example field definitions
- Shell completions and flag contracts updated

## Evidence

- src/core/schema/runtime-schema.ts:238-278 (normalizeRuntimeFieldDefinition — shows all field properties)
- src/core/schema/runtime-schema.ts:514-602 (loadRuntimeSchemaFromOptionalFiles — reads fields.json)
- src/cli/commands/schema.ts:42-51 (SCHEMA_SUBCOMMANDS — no field subcommands)
- src/cli/commands/schema.ts:163-187 (SchemaListResult — no fields array)
- src/core/schema/item-types-file.ts and status-defs-file.ts (implementation pattern to follow for fields-file.ts)
pm schema list-fields / show-field / add-field / remove-field: CLI management of schema/fields.json

Runtime custom fields (schema/fields.json) are the schema customization mechanism that lets projects add typed metadata columns with CLI flags, but the schema command family has no subcommands for them. Unlike types and statuses (which have add-type/remove-type/add-status/remove-status), custom fields require hand-editing schema/fields.json. pm schema list also doesn't display registered custom fields. This is a significant UX gap in the 'universal customization' story.
Let any project define project-specific metadata fields via CLI without editing JSON files by hand
Completes the schema/config universal-customization story by closing the last CLI-inaccessible schema primitive

- pm schema list includes a 'fields' section showing registered custom fields (key, type, commands, cli_flag)
- pm schema show-field <key> shows full field definition
- pm schema add-field <key> --type <type> --commands create,update --description <text> [--required] [--required-on-create] [--allow-unset] [--cli-flag <flag>] [--alias <alias>] manages schema/fields.json
- pm schema remove-field <key> removes entry from schema/fields.json with advisory usage count
- pm schema add-field is idempotent (upsert); warns if key shadows a builtin field name
- CONFIGURATION.md documents schema/fields.json format and example field definitions
- Shell completions and flag contracts updated
